brief_document.title: aion-store-libsql crate scaffold, config, and error mapping
brief_document.purpose: Create the aion-store-libsql leaf-of-the-store-tree crate, its Deserialize-only configuration, and the boundary error mapping into StoreError. This is the foundation every other brief in the cluster builds on: the crate exists, depends only on aion-store, and can express its modes and its errors.
brief_document.task: Create the aion-store-libsql crate depending only on aion-store among Aion crates (plus libsql, serde, thiserror, chrono, and async support). Write a thin lib.rs. Define LibSqlConfig as a Deserialize-only type whose mode enum is Embedded { path } or EmbeddedReplica { path, primary_url, auth_token }, exposing libSQL durability/WAL/sync settings with no hardcoded defaults. Define the error-mapping surface that converts libsql and serde errors into StoreError. Do NOT implement EventStore here — that begins in AS-003.

requirements.title: Crate scaffold and workspace wiring
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the aion-store-libsql crate as a workspace member whose only Aion dependency is aion-store. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT depend on the engine, beamr, or any other storage backend crate.
acceptance: crates/aion-store-libsql exists and is registered in the workspace Cargo.toml members list.

Cargo.toml declares aion-store as the only aion-* dependency, plus external crates: libsql, serde (with derive), thiserror, chrono, and async-trait (or relies on native async-fn-in-trait consistent with how aion-store declares EventStore).
src/lib.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic.
The crate sets unsafe_code = "deny" and inherits the workspace clippy lints.

requirements.title: LibSqlConfig mode enum
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define LibSqlConfig as a Deserialize-only configuration type whose mode is an enum with Embedded { path } and EmbeddedReplica { path, primary_url, auth_token } variants. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT derive Serialize on LibSqlConfig.
acceptance: config.rs defines LibSqlConfig deriving Deserialize and Debug but NOT Serialize.
The mode is an enum: Embedded { path: PathBuf } and EmbeddedReplica { path: PathBuf, primary_url: String, auth_token: String }.
A unit test deserialises a TOML or JSON fixture for each variant and asserts the parsed fields.
Embedded mode requires only a path; EmbeddedReplica additionally requires primary_url and auth_token (deserialising a replica config missing primary_url is an error).

requirements.title: Durability and sync settings, no assumed defaults
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose libSQL durability/WAL settings (and, for replica mode, a sync interval) on LibSqlConfig. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT bake hardcoded default values for these tunables into the crate; values absent from config SHALL be represented as unset (Option) and left to libSQL's own behaviour or surfaced for the operator, not silently defaulted by this crate.
acceptance: LibSqlConfig carries durability/WAL tunables (e.g. an Option for journal/WAL mode and an Option sync_interval used in replica mode) as Option fields, not bare values with crate-chosen defaults.
No literal default magic numbers for sync interval or WAL settings appear in the crate; absent config maps to None.
A unit test deserialises a config omitting the tunables and asserts the corresponding fields are None.
A doc comment states that operators set these; the crate does not assume values (per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults).

requirements.title: Boundary error mapping into StoreError
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a single boundary that maps libsql driver errors and serde (de)serialisation failures into StoreError. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow any libsql or serde error type to appear in the public EventStore-facing surface.
acceptance: error.rs defines conversions (From impls or helper fns) mapping libsql::Error into StoreError::Backend and serde_json errors into StoreError::Serialization.
The mapping preserves a human-readable message from the source error in the StoreError variant payload.
No public function signature in the crate returns or exposes a libsql or serde error type; all fallible store operations surface StoreError.
A unit test constructs a representative libsql error and a serde error and asserts each maps to the expected StoreError variant.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the EventStore trait — store wiring is AS-003.
SHALL NOT open a libSQL connection or create schema — that is AS-002.
SHALL NOT depend on any Aion crate other than aion-store.
SHALL NOT derive Serialize on LibSqlConfig — it is a Deserialize-only config type.
verification: grep confirms no Serialize derive on LibSqlConfig and no hardcoded default sync/WAL values.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: crates/aion-store-libsql exists, is a workspace member, and depends only on aion-store among Aion crates (plus external crates: libsql, serde, thiserror, chrono, async-trait or native async).
src/lib.rs contains only module declarations and re-exports, no logic.
LibSqlConfig is a Deserialize-only type whose mode enum selects Embedded { path } or EmbeddedReplica { path, primary_url, auth_token }.
LibSqlConfig exposes libSQL durability/WAL settings (e.g. sync interval for replica mode, journal/WAL options) with no hardcoded default values baked into the crate.

A boundary error-mapping module maps every libsql driver error and serde (de)serialisation failure into StoreError (Backend, Serialization), so no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

stories.text: As an engine operator, I want to tune libSQL durability and sync settings through configuration with no values silently assumed for me, so that I control the durability-versus-throughput trade-off for my deployment.
As a store author, I want every libSQL and serialisation error mapped into the StoreError taxonomy so that the engine handles one uniform error surface regardless of backend.

As a store author, I want libSQL's internals to be swappable for the Turso-native engine later without touching the engine, so that adopting the production-ready rewrite is a drop-in change when it leaves beta.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses per CLAUDE.md. Tests needing a remote primary use a runtime env-gate, not #[ignore].
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only; logic lives in named modules.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace). The EventStore impl is split across append, read, and timer modules.
Depends only on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) among Aion crates — no dependency on the engine, beamr, or any other backend. Structural; must hold.
Raw SQL is permitted here because this crate IS a storage backend (the storage layer). No other crate issues SQL against this store; they go through the EventStore trait.
WorkflowStatus is derived from event history via aion-core's projection. No mutable stored status field; a cached projection must never diverge from the events.
LibSqlStore must pass the shared AC-007 conformance suite unmodified — the same behavioural standard as the oracle.
append's sequence guard and event inserts execute in a single libSQL transaction; a mismatch writes nothing and returns SequenceConflict — no partial batch is ever persisted.
LibSqlConfig is Deserialize-only and carries no hardcoded durability/WAL defaults; tunable values are surfaced for the operator to set, per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults.
All libsql and serde errors are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary; no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

resolved_context.intention: When an engine operator embeds Aion and writes no storage configuration, this is the store they get: a single file on disk, zero external infrastructure, full durability. The same crate serves the distributed path — the local file becomes an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary — without the operator learning a second backend. The in-memory reference store (cluster AC) proves the contract but forgets on restart; LibSqlStore makes durability the easy default, held to the identical behavioural standard as the oracle. A workflow that sleeps for months and resumes needs its history to survive every crash and reboot in between. When this cluster is done, an operator writes LibSqlStore::open("app.db") and has a production-grade durable event store the engine cannot tell apart from the oracle except that it remembers; the shared AC-007 conformance suite passes against it unchanged, plus a durability-specific suite proving state survives close-and-reopen.
